keyPoints: ACID Properties: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability
Concurrent Transactions: Multiple transactions can run concurrently, but must be committed or rolled back together
Distributed Deadlock: A deadlock occurs when two or more transactions are blocked, each waiting for the other to release a resource
detailedPoints: 2PC Protocol: A transaction proposes a commit, waits for acknowledgement, then commits if all nodes agree
3PC Protocol: A transaction proposes a commit, waits for acknowledgement, then abandons if any node fails to commit
Resource Locks: Locking resources to prevent concurrent access, reducing the likelihood of deadlocks
recoveryPoints: Rollback Recovery: Reverts transactions to a previous state, restoring consistency
Checkpointing: Periodically saves the state of a transaction, allowing for faster recovery
